marketFocus: US-Iran talks in Islamabad collapsed after 21 hours. VP Vance's delegation returned to the US without an agreement — the sticking point was Iran's nuclear ambitions. US Central Command announced a naval blockade of all maritime traffic entering and leaving Iranian ports, effective 16:00 FFT today. Trump: 'any Iranians who fire at the US or at peaceful vessels will be blown to hell.' Brent crude surged back above $100/bbl. Euro Stoxx 50 futures -1.4%. DXY strengthened on haven demand.

The blockade is technically limited to Iranian ports — CENTCOM confirmed it will 'not impede' vessels transiting the Strait of Hormuz to or from non-Iranian ports. However, Iran's IRGC warned that any military vessel approaching the Strait is a breach of the ceasefire, and Iran's FM said they were 'just inches away from an Islamabad MoU' before the US shifted goalposts. A second round of talks could happen within days — Trump: 'Iran has not left the bargaining table.' 10yr JGB yield hit its highest since 1997 (2.49%) as higher oil stokes global inflation fears.

Today's calendar is light — no high-impact red folder events. Speakers: ECB's de Guindos, RBA's Hauser, Fed's Miran. Earnings: Goldman Sachs, LVMH. Key risk: any escalation at 16:00 FFT when the blockade goes live. AUD Employment (Thu 03:30 FFT) is the next major data catalyst — positions in AUD pairs should be managed from 16:00 FFT Wednesday (TONIGHT badge activates).

USD.tone: Hormuz Blockade Restores Haven Premium — DXY Strengthened; Stagflation Framework Back
USD.summary: USD is Bullish today. The collapse of US-Iran talks and Trump's Hormuz blockade announcement have fully restored the USD's stagflation haven premium. DXY strengthened as risk-off swept through Asia and Europe — the same dynamic that drove USD higher in the early weeks of the conflict is back in play. Oil above $100/bbl means the Fed's conditional hawkish hold ('higher oil could justify rate rises') is now relevant again. Treasury yields climbed as oil-driven inflation fears returned. Weekly Weak Bearish + Daily Bullish = ❌ NO alignment — intraday only.
drivers: Hormuz blockade restores stagflation haven premium — DXY strengthened on risk-off
Brent back above $100/bbl — oil-driven inflation fears reignite Fed hike repricing
10yr UST yields climbed as oil surge stoked inflationary concerns
Trump: 'fully locked and loaded, military will finish up the little that is left of Iran'
Weekly Weak Bearish + Daily Bullish = NO alignment — intraday only

EUR.tone: EUR/USD Slipped Below 1.17 — Energy Importer Hurt by Oil Surge; EU Gas Up Double Digits
EUR.summary: EUR is Bearish today. EUR/USD slipped back below the 1.1700 handle as the failed US-Iran talks and Hormuz blockade drove EU gas futures higher by double-digit percentages. The Eurozone's status as a net energy importer is the dominant headwind — the same structural vulnerability that weighed on EUR in the early conflict weeks is back. Bund futures fell below 125.00. ECB de Guindos speaks today — any hawkish tone would provide a short-term bounce but cannot offset the energy shock narrative. Weekly Weak Bullish + Daily Bearish = ❌ NO alignment — intraday only.
drivers: EUR/USD slipped below 1.1700 — energy importer hurt by oil surge and EU gas up double digits
Bund futures fell below 125.00 — EU energy shock feeding bond sell-off
ECB de Guindos speaks today — watch for tone, but energy headwind dominates

GBP.tone: GBP/USD Struggling to Hold 1.34 — Risk-Off Cyclical Pressure; S&P Affirmed AA Stable
GBP.summary: GBP is Bearish today. GBP/USD is struggling to hold the 1.3400 level amid broad risk-off pressure and cyclical headwinds from the Hormuz blockade. Sterling's risk-sensitive character means it suffers in genuine risk-off environments despite the BOE hawkish repricing story. S&P affirmed the UK at AA with a stable outlook — a mild positive but insufficient to shift the bias. UK PM Starmer is pushing EU alignment legislation, which is structurally positive for trade but not a near-term FX catalyst. Weekly Weak Bullish + Daily Bearish = ❌ NO alignment — intraday only.

JPY.tone: 10yr JGB Yield at 1997 High (2.49%) — Higher Oil Headwind for Japan; USD/JPY Edging Higher
JPY.summary: JPY is Bearish today. Japan's 10yr JGB yield hit 2.49% — its highest since 1997 — as higher oil prices stoked global inflation fears and forced a bond sell-off. USD/JPY edged higher as the greenback strengthened on haven demand. Japan's energy import vulnerability means higher oil is directly negative for the current account and growth outlook, muddling the BOJ policy picture. BOJ Governor Ueda said the economy is 'recovering moderately but with some weakness' and that underlying inflation is 'gradually accelerating toward the target' — no fresh hawkish signal. Weekly Bearish + Daily Bearish = ✅ HIGH CONVICTION alignment, but no bullish aligned pair to match against — intraday only.

USD/JPY edging higher — DXY strength + Japan energy import vulnerability

Japan Economy Minister Akazawa: BOJ policy to boost yen 'could be an option' — verbal support
Weekly Bearish + Daily Bearish = HIGH CONVICTION alignment — intraday only (no bullish pair)

AUD.tone: Antipodeans Under Pressure — ASX 200 Dragged Lower; Copper Fell on Failed Talks
AUD.summary: AUD is Bearish today. The Antipodeans declined sharply as the failed US-Iran talks and Hormuz blockade triggered a broad risk-off move. ASX 200 was dragged lower by underperformance in tech and miners, while energy was the only sector in the green. Copper futures declined following the lack of a breakthrough in talks — a direct negative for AUD via the commodity channel. AUD/USD is finding support at the 0.7000 level but the bias is clearly bearish. RBA's Hauser speaks today — watch for any shift in tone. AUD Employment on Thursday (03:30 FFT) is the key domestic catalyst — TONIGHT badge activates from 16:00 FFT Wednesday. Weekly Weak Bullish + Daily Bearish = ❌ NO alignment — intraday only.

CAD.tone: Oil Back Above $100/bbl — Petro-Proxy Support Fully Restored; Hormuz Blockade Bullish CAD
CAD.summary: CAD is Bullish today. The Hormuz blockade and failed US-Iran talks have driven Brent back above $100/bbl — directly restoring the petro-proxy support that was CAD's primary tailwind before last week's ceasefire-driven collapse. Saudi Arabia restored the East-West pipeline to full capacity (7mln bpd), but this is insufficient to offset the Hormuz supply shock. CAD is the clearest beneficiary of the oil surge among G10 currencies. BOC Governor Macklem speaks Tuesday — watch for any shift in tone given the oil reversal. Weekly Weak Bearish + Daily Bullish = ❌ NO alignment — intraday only.

CHF.tone: Asymmetric Haven — Lagging USD in Risk-Off; SNB Intervention Cap Limits Upside
CHF.summary: CHF is Neutral today. The Franc's asymmetric haven behavior is once again on display — it is benefiting modestly from risk-off but significantly lagging USD, which is the market's preferred defensive currency given its yield advantage. The SNB intervention cap limits excessive CHF strength, and the market has consistently chosen USD over CHF in this conflict cycle. Neither a strong positive nor negative driver dominates. SNB Chairman Schlegel speaks Wednesday — the key event for CHF this week.
drivers: Asymmetric haven — benefiting modestly from risk-off but lagging USD significantly
SNB intervention cap limits excessive CHF strength
USD preferred as haven over CHF — yield advantage and intervention risk

NZD.tone: NZD/USD Holding 0.58 Support — Risk-Off Pressure Overrides RBNZ Hawkish Story
NZD.summary: NZD is Bearish today. The Antipodeans declined as the failed US-Iran talks and Hormuz blockade triggered a broad risk-off move. NZD/USD is finding support at the 0.5800 level, but the risk-off environment is overriding the RBNZ hawkish story that drove NZD higher last week. RBNZ Governor Breman said the central bank 'will soon announce steps to increase transparency of monetary policy decisions' — a procedural comment, not a fresh hawkish signal. Breman speaks Wednesday — the key catalyst for NZD this week. Weekly Bullish + Daily Bearish = ❌ NO alignment — intraday only.
